text: When setting project goals, it's important to make them SMART: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." Break big goals into smaller milestones, like completing the homepage design by week two and launching the full site by week ten. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, such as a design mockup or a report. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, like clients or team members. This way, everyone agrees on what success looks like and works toward the same outcome.
